82	    workspace_admin_emails: str = ""
83	
84	    secret_store: Literal["env", "redis", "azure_key_vault"] = (
85	        "env"  # noqa: S105 — mode selector, not a password
86	    )
87	    azure_key_vault_url: str | None = None
88	
89	    # SecretStore key holding the ADF webhook shared secret (ADR 0006). Resolved
90	    # via SecretStore.get → EnvSecretStore maps it to KV_SECRET_ADF_WEBHOOK_SECRET
91	    # in dev, Key Vault secret `adf-webhook-secret` in prod. Not the secret value.
92	    adf_webhook_secret_name: str = "adf-webhook-secret"  # noqa: S105 — KV key name, not a secret
93	    # SecretStore key holding the Airflow callback HMAC signing key (ADR 0007).
94	    # → KV_SECRET_AIRFLOW_WEBHOOK_SECRET in dev, KV secret `airflow-webhook-secret`
95	    # in prod. The signing key, not a webhook value.
96	    airflow_webhook_secret_name: str = "airflow-webhook-secret"  # noqa: S105 — KV key name
97	    # SecretStore key holding the dbt callback HMAC signing key (ADR 0029; sibling
98	    # of the Airflow key). → KV_SECRET_DBT_WEBHOOK_SECRET in dev, KV secret
99	    # `dbt-webhook-secret` in prod. App-level (shared across dbt connections); the
100	    # per-connection secret is the artifacts-store read credential, not this.
101	    dbt_webhook_secret_name: str = "dbt-webhook-secret"  # noqa: S105 — KV key name
102	
103	    # SecretStore key holding the workspace MS Teams incoming-webhook URL (the URL
104	    # carries a token, so it lives in the SecretStore, not in config). Unset →
105	    # no Teams alerting (the no-op publisher). The value is the webhook URL,
106	    # resolved per run via SecretStore so a rotated webhook is picked up;
107	    # per-suite notification config (a later PR) extends the resolver. Provider-
108	    # neutral: Teams is one ResultPublisher impl behind the registry (ADR 0011).
109	    teams_webhook_secret_name: str | None = None
110	
111	    # SSRF allowlist for the per-suite Teams webhook URL. The webhook is supplied
112	    # by a suite editor and POSTed server-side, so its host is constrained to this
113	    # comma-separated set of suffixes (defaults to MS Teams incoming-webhook +
114	    # Power Automate workflow hosts; extend via env for a private relay). Stored as
115	    # a string — not list[str] — like workspace_admin_emails, to sidestep Pydantic
116	    # env list-parsing.
117	    teams_webhook_allowed_hosts: str = "webhook.office.com,logic.azure.com"
118	
119	    # ── Slack alerting (workspace-level incoming webhook) ────────────────────
120	    # SecretStore key holding the Slack incoming-webhook URL
121	    # (https://hooks.slack.com/services/...). The URL carries a token, so it
122	    # lives in the SecretStore. Unset → no Slack alerting (quiet no-op). Resolved
123	    # per run so a rotated webhook is picked up. One ResultPublisher impl behind
124	    # the registry composite (ADR 0011) — same per-suite alert_on policy as Teams.
125	    slack_webhook_secret_name: str | None = None
126	    # SSRF allowlist for the Slack webhook host (POSTed server-side).
127	    slack_webhook_allowed_hosts: str = "hooks.slack.com"
128	
129	    # ── Email (SMTP) alerting ────────────────────────────────────────────────
130	    # Non-secret SMTP coordinates live in config; the password (e.g. a Gmail
131	    # app-password) lives in the SecretStore by name. Email alerting is active
132	    # only when email_to, email_username, and email_password_secret_name are all
133	    # set (else a quiet no-op). STARTTLS on the submission port.
134	    email_smtp_host: str = "smtp.gmail.com"
135	    email_smtp_port: int = 587
136	    email_username: str | None = None
137	    email_from: str | None = None  # defaults to email_username when unset
138	    email_to: str = ""  # comma-separated recipients; empty → no email alerting
139	    email_password_secret_name: str | None = None
140	
141	    # ── Snowflake probe (Week 1 exit-gate endpoint) ──────────────────────────
142	    # Config for the single seeded dev Snowflake connection the probe runs
143	    # against. All optional: when unset the probe still creates + dispatches a
144	    # run, which then fails-soft (no live warehouse). secret_ref names the
145	    # SecretStore entry holding the password (e.g. KV_SECRET_SNOWFLAKE_DEV).
146	    probe_snowflake_account: str | None = None
147	    probe_snowflake_user: str | None = None
148	    probe_snowflake_database: str | None = None
149	    probe_snowflake_schema: str | None = None
150	    probe_snowflake_warehouse: str | None = None
151	    probe_snowflake_role: str | None = None
152	    probe_snowflake_table: str | None = None
153	    probe_snowflake_secret_ref: str | None = None
154	
155	    @property
156	    def workspace_admin_email_set(self) -> frozenset[str]:
157	        """Normalised (lower-cased, stripped) admin emails for membership tests.
158	
159	        Empty when unset → no workspace admins (every /admin request 403s), the
160	        safe default.
161	        """
162	        return frozenset(
163	            part.strip().lower() for part in self.workspace_admin_emails.split(",") if part.strip()
164	        )
165	
166	    def is_admin_email(self, email: str | None) -> bool:
167	        """True iff `email` is in the workspace-admin allowlist. The one
168	        normalization (strip + lower, null-safe) both the REST gate
169	        (`core.auth.is_workspace_admin`) and the per-suite gate (`suite_authz`)
170	        share — so the two can't drift."""
171	        normalized = (email or "").strip().lower()
172	        return bool(normalized) and normalized in self.workspace_admin_email_set
173	
174	    @property
175	    def cors_allow_origin_list(self) -> list[str]:
176	        """Parsed CORS origins (stripped, empties dropped). Empty → CORS off."""
177	        return [o.strip() for o in self.cors_allow_origins.split(",") if o.strip()]
178	
179	    @property
180	    def azure_auth_configured(self) -> bool:
181	        return bool(self.azure_tenant_id and self.azure_api_client_id)
182	
183	    @property
184	    def azure_api_scope_uri(self) -> str | None:
185	        if not self.azure_api_client_id:
186	            return None
187	        return f"api://{self.azure_api_client_id}/{self.azure_api_scope}"
188

190	@lru_cache(maxsize=1)
191	def get_settings() -> Settings:
192	    return Settings()

151	@pytest.fixture
152	def _restore_root_logging() -> Iterator[None]:
153	    """Snapshot/restore the root logger so the App Insights integration test below
154	    doesn't leak its handler into the rest of the suite."""
155	    root = std_logging.getLogger()
156	    saved_handlers, saved_level = root.handlers[:], root.level
157	    yield

164	class _FakeAzureLogHandler(std_logging.Handler):
165	    """Stand-in for opencensus's AzureLogHandler that reproduces ONLY the bug-
166	    relevant behaviour — `createLock()` nulls `self.lock` — with no network,
167	    statsbeat thread, or App Insights export. Lets the test drive the real
168	    `configure_logging()` code path (so it catches a revert of the fix) without
169	    constructing the real, network-touching handler."""
170	
171	    def __init__(self, connection_string: str) -> None:
172	        super().__init__()  # stdlib __init__ calls self.createLock() -> nulls it
173	
174	    def createLock(self) -> None:
175	        self.lock = None  # mirrors opencensus's override
176	
177	
178	def test_azure_handler_lock_survives_createlock_recall(
179	    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, _restore_root_logging: None
180	) -> None:
181	    """#405 (a #393 recurrence): Celery's embedded beat (`worker -B`) re-initialises
182	    logging in its forked process and calls the AzureLogHandler's createLock() again.
183	    opencensus's createLock sets `self.lock = None`; on Py3.13 that makes
184	    logging.Handler.handle()'s `with self.lock` crash on the first record — killing
185	    beat (and every periodic task) on its 'beat: Starting...' line. The handler must
186	    keep a real lock no matter how often createLock() is called.
187	
188	    Uses a fake handler (above) so configure_logging()'s override is exercised
189	    without real opencensus network/statsbeat behaviour."""
190	    import opencensus.ext.azure.log_exporter as az_log_exporter
191	
192	    monkeypatch.setattr(az_log_exporter, "AzureLogHandler", _FakeAzureLogHandler)
193	    monkeypatch.setattr(
194	        get_settings(),
195	        "applicationinsights_connection_string",
196	        "InstrumentationKey=00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",
197	    )
198	    configure_logging()
199	    ai = [h for h in std_logging.getLogger().handlers if isinstance(h, _FakeAzureLogHandler)]
200	    assert ai, "App Insights handler was not attached when a connection string is set"
201	    handler = ai[0]
202	
203	    handler.createLock()  # simulate the beat fork re-initialising logging
204	    assert handler.lock is not None, "createLock() re-nulled the lock — beat would crash"
205	
206	    # The exact prod crash path: handle() acquires `with self.lock`. Don't export.
207	    monkeypatch.setattr(handler, "emit", lambda record: None)
208	    record = std_logging.LogRecord(
209	        "celery.beat", std_logging.INFO, __file__, 1, "beat: Starting...", None, None
210	    )
211	    handler.handle(record)  # must not raise
212	
213	
214	def test_app_insights_handler_redacts_foreign_record_secret(
215	    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, _restore_root_logging: None
216	) -> None:
217	    """#494: the AzureLogHandler must carry the redacting ProcessorFormatter, so a
218	    FOREIGN (non-structlog) record whose message embeds a secret (e.g. a URL with
219	    ?token=) is scrubbed before it reaches App Insights — not just the stdout path."""
220	    import opencensus.ext.azure.log_exporter as az_log_exporter
221	
222	    monkeypatch.setattr(az_log_exporter, "AzureLogHandler", _FakeAzureLogHandler)
223	    monkeypatch.setattr(
224	        get_settings(),
225	        "applicationinsights_connection_string",
226	        "InstrumentationKey=00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",
227	    )
228	    configure_logging()
229	    ai = next(h for h in std_logging.getLogger().handlers if isinstance(h, _FakeAzureLogHandler))
230	    assert ai.formatter is not None, "AI handler must use the redacting ProcessorFormatter"
231	
232	    record = std_logging.LogRecord(
233	        "uvicorn.error",
234	        std_logging.INFO,
235	        __file__,
236	        1,
237	        'GET /api/v1/orchestration/events/adf?token=SUPERSECRET-1 HTTP/1.1" 200',
238	        None,
239	        None,
240	    )

242	    assert "SUPERSECRET-1" not in rendered
243	    assert "token=<redacted>" in rendered

263	def test_exception_tracebacks_drop_frame_locals_and_scrub_messages(
264	    capsys: pytest.CaptureFixture[str],
265	) -> None:
266	    """#536: frame locals (which can hold anything in scope — engine URLs with
267	    embedded credentials, sample rows) must NOT be serialized into the log
268	    event, and the rendered exception strings pass the scrubber."""
269	    configure_logging()

1	"""Tests for request/task span instrumentation (WEEK7 A3).
2	
3	The Azure exporter is stubbed with the SDK's InMemorySpanExporter so the whole
4	configure → instrument → span-emit path runs for real without any network.
5	`trace.set_tracer_provider` is process-global and set-once, so the autouse
6	fixture no-ops it (both instrumentors receive the provider explicitly via
7	``tracer_provider=``, so nothing here depends on the global) and shuts the
8	provider down on teardown — no cross-test poisoning, no leaked export thread.
9	"""
10	
11	import importlib
12	from types import SimpleNamespace
13	from typing import Any
14	
15	import pytest
16	from fastapi import FastAPI
17	from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
18	from opentelemetry.sdk.trace.export.in_memory_span_exporter import InMemorySpanExporter
19	
20	from backend.app.core import tracing
21	from backend.app.core.config import get_settings
22	
23	_CONN = "InstrumentationKey=00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
24	
25	
26	@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
27	def _isolate_tracing_state(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> Any:
28	    monkeypatch.setattr(tracing, "_provider", None)
29	    monkeypatch.setattr("opentelemetry.trace.set_tracer_provider", lambda provider: None)
30	    yield
31	    if tracing._provider is not None:
32	        tracing._provider.shutdown()
33	
34	
35	@pytest.fixture()
36	def in_memory_exporter(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> InMemorySpanExporter:
37	    """Swap the Azure exporter for an in-memory one; enable tracing settings."""
38	    exporter = InMemorySpanExporter()
39	    monkeypatch.setattr(
40	        "azure.monitor.opentelemetry.exporter.AzureMonitorTraceExporter",
41	        lambda *, connection_string: exporter,
42	    )
43	    monkeypatch.setattr(
44	        tracing,
45	        "get_settings",
46	        lambda: SimpleNamespace(applicationinsights_connection_string=_CONN),
47	    )
48	    return exporter
49	
50	
51	def _server_span_routes(exporter: InMemorySpanExporter) -> set[Any]:
52	    assert tracing._provider is not None
53	    tracing._provider.force_flush()
54	    return {
55	        s.attributes.get("http.route")
56	        for s in exporter.get_finished_spans()
57	        if s.kind.name == "SERVER" and s.attributes
58	    }
59

73	def test_instrumentors_and_tagger_are_noops_when_tracing_off() -> None:
74	    """With no provider configured, instrument_*/tag_request_id must not wrap anything."""
75	    app = FastAPI()
76	
77	    @app.get("/api/v1/ping")
78	    def ping() -> dict[str, str]:
79	        tracing.tag_request_id("rid-1")  # must not raise with tracing off
80	        return {"pong": "yes"}

86	    assert not getattr(app, "_is_instrumented_by_opentelemetry", False)

89	def test_spans_emitted_and_excluded_urls_and_query_scrubbed(
90	    in_memory_exporter: InMemorySpanExporter,
91	) -> None:
92	    """Enabled path: span per API request; none for /healthz or the webhook
93	    (its ?token= must never reach attributes, #494); query strings scrubbed
94	    from URL attributes; request_id tag helper works inside a request."""
95	    tracing.configure_tracing(service_name="dataq-api")
96	    assert tracing._provider is not None
97	
98	    app = FastAPI()
99	
100	    @app.get("/api/v1/ping")
101	    def ping() -> dict[str, str]:
102	        tracing.tag_request_id("rid-42")
103	        return {"pong": "yes"}
104	
105	    @app.get("/healthz")
106	    def healthz() -> dict[str, str]:
107	        return {"status": "ok"}
108	
109	    @app.post("/api/v1/orchestration/events/adf")
110	    def adf_webhook() -> dict[str, str]:
111	        return {"received": "yes"}
112	
113	    tracing.instrument_fastapi(app)
114	    client = TestClient(app)
115	    assert client.get("/api/v1/ping?q=olivia@corp.example").status_code == 200
116	    assert client.get("/healthz").status_code == 200
117	    assert client.post("/api/v1/orchestration/events/adf?token=supersecret").status_code == 200
118	
119	    routes = _server_span_routes(in_memory_exporter)

result.output:
-rw-r--r--@ 1 arijit  admin  35028 Jul  1 16:02 /opt/homebrew/Caskroom/miniconda/base/envs/dataq/lib/python3.13/site-packages/opentelemetry/sdk/_logs/_internal/__init__.py
class LoggingHandler(logging.Handler):
    """A handler class which writes logging records, in OTLP format, to
    a network destination or file. Supports signals from the `logging` module.
    https://docs.python.org/3/library/logging.html
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        level: int = logging.NOTSET,
        logger_provider: APILoggerProvider | None = None,
    ) -> None:
        super().__init__(level=level)
        self._logger_provider = logger_provider or get_logger_provider()

        warnings.warn(
            "`LoggingHandler` in `opentelemetry-sdk` is deprecated. Use the "
            "handler from `opentelemetry-instrumentation-logging` instead.",
            DeprecationWarning,
        )

    @staticmethod
    def _get_attributes(record: logging.LogRecord) -> _ExtendedAttributes:
        attributes = {
            k: v for k, v in vars(record).items() if k not in _RESERVED_ATTRS
        }

        # Add standard code attributes for logs.
        attributes[code_attributes.CODE_FILE_PATH] = record.pathname
        attributes[code_attributes.CODE_FUNCTION_NAME] = record.funcName
        attributes[code_attributes.CODE_LINE_NUMBER] = record.lineno

        if record.exc_info:
            exctype, value, tb = record.exc_info
            if exctype is not None:
                attributes[exception_attributes.EXCEPTION_TYPE] = (
                    exctype.__name__
                )
            if value is not None and value.args:
                attributes[exception_attributes.EXCEPTION_MESSAGE] = str(
                    value.args[0]
                )
            if tb is not None:
                # https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/exceptions/exceptions-spans/#stacktrace-representation
                attributes[exception_attributes.EXCEPTION_STACKTRACE] = (
                    "".join(traceback.format_exception(*record.exc_info))
                )
        return attributes

    def _translate(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> LogRecord:
        timestamp = int(record.created * 1e9)
        observered_timestamp = time_ns()
        attributes = self._get_attributes(record)
        severity_number = std_to_otel(record.levelno)
        if self.formatter:
            body = self.format(record)
        else:
            # `record.getMessage()` uses `record.msg` as a template to format
            # `record.args` into. There is a special case in `record.getMessage()`
            # where it will only attempt formatting if args are provided,
            # otherwise, it just stringifies `record.msg`.
            #
            # Since the OTLP body field has a type of 'any' and the logging module
            # is sometimes used in such a way that objects incorrectly end up
            # set as record.msg, in those cases we would like to bypass
            # `record.getMessage()` completely and set the body to the object
            # itself instead of its string representation.
            # For more background, see: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-python/pull/4216
            if not record.args and not isinstance(record.msg, str):
                #  if record.msg is not a value we can export, cast it to string
                if not isinstance(record.msg, _VALID_ANY_VALUE_TYPES):
                    body = str(record.msg)
                else:
                    body = record.msg
            else:
                body = record.getMessage()

        # Map Python log level names to OTel severity text as defined in
        # https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/logs/data-model.md#displaying-severity
        # Python "WARNING" -> OTel "WARN" (see #3548)
        # Python "CRITICAL" -> OTel "FATAL" (see #4984)
        _python_to_otel_severity_text = {
            "WARNING": "WARN",
            "CRITICAL": "FATAL",
        }
        level_name = _python_to_otel_severity_text.get(
            record.levelname, record.levelname
        )

        return LogRecord(
            timestamp=timestamp,
            observed_timestamp=observered_timestamp,
            context=get_current() or None,
            severity_text=level_name,
            severity_number=severity_number,
            body=body,
            attributes=attributes,
        )

    def emit(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> None:
        """
        Emit a record. Skip emitting if logger is NoOp.

        The record is translated to OTel format, and then sent across the pipeline.
        """
        logger = get_logger(record.name, logger_provider=self._logger_provider)
        if not isinstance(logger, NoOpLogger):
            logger.emit(self._translate(record))

    def flush(self) -> None:
        """
        Flushes the logging output. Skip flushing if logging_provider has no force_flush method.
        """
        if hasattr(self._logger_provider, "force_flush") and callable(
            self._logger_provider.force_flush  # type: ignore[reportAttributeAccessIssue]
        ):
            # This is done in a separate thread to avoid a potential deadlock, for
            # details see https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-python/pull/4636.
            thread = threading.Thread(target=self._logger_provider.force_flush)  # type: ignore[reportAttributeAccessIssue]
            thread.start()

content.text:
Two decisive findings from the source:

1. **Body redaction survives** — `if self.formatter: body = self.format(record)`. Attaching the existing redacting `ProcessorFormatter` keeps bodies scrubbed (both structlog and foreign records). ✓
2. **New attribute-leak vector** — `_get_attributes` copies *every* non-reserved record var into OTel attributes verbatim, bypassing the formatter. The old opencensus handler didn't do this. I must override `_get_attributes` to run it through the scrubber, or a foreign `extra=` could ship a secret un-redacted.

content.text: Now the shared exporter seam module:

input.content:
"""Shared OpenTelemetry exporter resolution — the vendor-neutral observability seam (ADR 0010).

Both signal pipelines resolve their backends here from settings so they can't
drift:

- **tracing.py** — request/task spans (a ``BatchSpanProcessor`` per exporter).
- **logging.py** — the structlog → stdlib → OTel log bridge (#524, which replaced
  the EOL opencensus ``AzureLogHandler``).

Two exporters sit behind the seam and **may both be active at once**:

- **Azure Monitor** — when ``APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING`` is set.
- **Generic OTLP/HTTP** — when ``OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`` is set (#589): spans
  and logs also export to any OTLP consumer (Grafana/Tempo, Jaeger, Datadog, …).
  Base-endpoint semantics per the OTel spec — ``/v1/traces`` and ``/v1/logs`` are
  appended to the configured base. Running both at once is exactly the parity
  check (the same trace/log lands in App Insights AND a local collector).

Neither set ⇒ telemetry is a complete no-op (the pre-#524 ``AzureLogHandler`` gate).

All exporter imports are **lazy** (repo convention, see ``secrets.py``) so a
deployment that uses neither backend never pays the import cost.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

from backend.app.core.config import Settings, get_settings

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from opentelemetry.sdk._logs.export import LogExporter
    from opentelemetry.sdk.resources import Resource
    from opentelemetry.sdk.trace.export import SpanExporter


def _otlp_base(settings: Settings) -> str | None:
    """The OTLP base endpoint (no trailing slash), or None when OTLP is off."""
    endpoint = settings.otel_exporter_otlp_endpoint
    return endpoint.rstrip("/") if endpoint else None


def telemetry_enabled(settings: Settings | None = None) -> bool:
    """True when at least one exporter backend is configured (Azure or OTLP)."""
    settings = settings or get_settings()
    return bool(settings.applicationinsights_connection_string) or bool(_otlp_base(settings))


def build_resource(service_name: str) -> Resource:
    """The OTel Resource carrying ``service.name`` (App Insights cloud role name)."""
    from opentelemetry.sdk.resources import Resource

    return Resource.create({"service.name": service_name})


def build_span_exporters(settings: Settings | None = None) -> list[SpanExporter]:
    """The configured span exporters — Azure Monitor and/or generic OTLP/HTTP."""
    settings = settings or get_settings()
    exporters: list[SpanExporter] = []

    conn = settings.applicationinsights_connection_string
    if conn:
        from azure.monitor.opentelemetry.exporter import AzureMonitorTraceExporter

        exporters.append(AzureMonitorTraceExporter(connection_string=conn))

    base = _otlp_base(settings)
    if base:
        from opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.http.trace_exporter import OTLPSpanExporter

        exporters.append(OTLPSpanExporter(endpoint=f"{base}/v1/traces"))

    return exporters


def build_log_exporters(settings: Settings | None = None) -> list[LogExporter]:
    """The configured log exporters — Azure Monitor and/or generic OTLP/HTTP."""
    settings = settings or get_settings()
    exporters: list[LogExporter] = []

    conn = settings.applicationinsights_connection_string
    if conn:
        from azure.monitor.opentelemetry.exporter import AzureMonitorLogExporter

        exporters.append(AzureMonitorLogExporter(connection_string=conn))

    base = _otlp_base(settings)
    if base:
        from opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.http._log_exporter import OTLPLogExporter

        exporters.append(OTLPLogExporter(endpoint=f"{base}/v1/logs"))

    return exporters

input.content:
"""Shared OpenTelemetry exporter resolution — the vendor-neutral observability seam (ADR 0010).

Both signal pipelines resolve their backends here from settings so they can't
drift:

- **tracing.py** — request/task spans (a ``BatchSpanProcessor`` per exporter).
- **logging.py** — the structlog → stdlib → OTel log bridge (#524, which replaced
  the EOL opencensus ``AzureLogHandler``).

The exporter behind the seam is **Azure Monitor** — configured when
``APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING`` is set; unset ⇒ telemetry is a complete
no-op (the pre-#524 ``AzureLogHandler`` gate). A generic OTLP/HTTP exporter joins
it behind the same seam in #589.

All exporter imports are **lazy** (repo convention, see ``secrets.py``) so a
deployment with telemetry off never pays the import cost.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

from backend.app.core.config import Settings, get_settings

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from opentelemetry.sdk._logs.export import LogRecordExporter
    from opentelemetry.sdk.resources import Resource
    from opentelemetry.sdk.trace.export import SpanExporter


def telemetry_enabled(settings: Settings | None = None) -> bool:
    """True when at least one exporter backend is configured."""
    settings = settings or get_settings()
    return bool(settings.applicationinsights_connection_string)


def build_resource(service_name: str) -> Resource:
    """The OTel Resource carrying ``service.name`` (App Insights cloud role name)."""
    from opentelemetry.sdk.resources import Resource

    return Resource.create({"service.name": service_name})


def build_span_exporters(settings: Settings | None = None) -> list[SpanExporter]:
    """The configured span exporters (Azure Monitor for now; OTLP joins in #589)."""
    settings = settings or get_settings()
    exporters: list[SpanExporter] = []

    conn = settings.applicationinsights_connection_string
    if conn:
        from azure.monitor.opentelemetry.exporter import AzureMonitorTraceExporter

        exporters.append(AzureMonitorTraceExporter(connection_string=conn))

    return exporters


def build_log_exporters(settings: Settings | None = None) -> list[LogRecordExporter]:
    """The configured log exporters (Azure Monitor for now; OTLP joins in #589)."""
    settings = settings or get_settings()
    exporters: list[LogRecordExporter] = []

    conn = settings.applicationinsights_connection_string
    if conn:
        from azure.monitor.opentelemetry.exporter import AzureMonitorLogExporter

        exporters.append(AzureMonitorLogExporter(connection_string=conn))

    return exporters

def _configure_otel_log_export(
    root: logging.Logger,
    level: int,
    formatter: logging.Formatter,
    service_name: str,
) -> None:
    """Bridge stdlib logging → OpenTelemetry → the configured exporter(s) (#524).

    Replaces the EOL opencensus ``AzureLogHandler`` (and its Py3.13 ``createLock``
    hardening, #393/#405). A **no-op** when no exporter is configured, matching the
    old connection-string gate. **Fork-safe**: the SDK's ``BatchLogRecordProcessor``
    re-inits its export thread in forked children (celery prefork) via
    ``os.register_at_fork``, so attaching here — exactly where the old handler
    attached — is correct in the worker as well as the API.

    The lazy imports (repo convention, ``secrets.py``) keep telemetry-off
    deployments from paying the OTel-logs import cost.
    """
    exporters = otel.build_log_exporters(settings=get_settings())
    if not exporters:
        return

    import warnings

    from opentelemetry._logs import set_logger_provider
    from opentelemetry.sdk._logs import LoggerProvider, LoggingHandler
    from opentelemetry.sdk._logs.export import BatchLogRecordProcessor

    class _RedactingOTelLogHandler(LoggingHandler):
        """OTel bridge that redacts the EXPORTED ATTRIBUTES, not just the body.

        The body is already scrubbed — ``LoggingHandler._translate`` renders it
        through our redacting ``ProcessorFormatter`` (``if self.formatter:``). But
        the base ``_get_attributes`` copies every non-reserved log-record var into
        the exported OTel attributes verbatim, *bypassing the formatter* — so a
        foreign record's ``extra=`` (or a library's custom record attribute) could
        ship a secret / PII to the backend un-redacted. Run those attributes
        through the same PII/secret scrubber the formatter applies to the body
        (#494/#536). This is stricter than the old opencensus handler, which only
        exported the formatted message."""

        def _get_attributes(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> Any:
            return _redact_pii(None, "", super()._get_attributes(record))

    provider = LoggerProvider(resource=otel.build_resource(service_name))
    for exporter in exporters:
        provider.add_log_record_processor(BatchLogRecordProcessor(exporter))
    set_logger_provider(provider)

    with warnings.catch_warnings():
        # The sdk `LoggingHandler` carries a DeprecationWarning nudging toward
        # `opentelemetry-instrumentation-logging` — which only injects trace
        # context into records; it is NOT an export bridge. The sdk handler IS
        # the bridge (it's what the azure-monitor distro uses under the hood).
        # Suppress the nudge so it neither spams stdout nor trips `-W error`.
        warnings.simplefilter("ignore", DeprecationWarning)
        handler = _RedactingOTelLogHandler(level=level, logger_provider=provider)
    # Same redacting ProcessorFormatter as stdout, so records exported to the
    # backend pass through `_redact_pii` (incl. the secret-string scrubber) — a
    # foreign record carrying a secret in its message is scrubbed before export
    # (#494). App-level structlog records are already redacted upstream.
    handler.setFormatter(formatter)
    root.addHandler(handler)


def configure_logging(service_name: str = "dataq") -> None:
    settings = get_settings()
    level = getattr(logging, settings.log_level)

content.text: Confirmed: body scrubbing needs my formatter, and `password` leaks as an attribute (my override fixes it), and the handler keeps a real lock (#405 class gone). Now rewrite the opencensus tests as OTel equivalents:

input.old_string:
class _FakeAzureLogHandler(std_logging.Handler):
    """Stand-in for opencensus's AzureLogHandler that reproduces ONLY the bug-
    relevant behaviour — `createLock()` nulls `self.lock` — with no network,
    statsbeat thread, or App Insights export. Lets the test drive the real
    `configure_logging()` code path (so it catches a revert of the fix) without
    constructing the real, network-touching handler."""

    def __init__(self, connection_string: str) -> None:
        super().__init__()  # stdlib __init__ calls self.createLock() -> nulls it

    def createLock(self) -> None:
        self.lock = None  # mirrors opencensus's override


def test_azure_handler_lock_survives_createlock_recall(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, _restore_root_logging: None
) -> None:
    """#405 (a #393 recurrence): Celery's embedded beat (`worker -B`) re-initialises
    logging in its forked process and calls the AzureLogHandler's createLock() again.
    opencensus's createLock sets `self.lock = None`; on Py3.13 that makes
    logging.Handler.handle()'s `with self.lock` crash on the first record — killing
    beat (and every periodic task) on its 'beat: Starting...' line. The handler must
    keep a real lock no matter how often createLock() is called.

    Uses a fake handler (above) so configure_logging()'s override is exercised
    without real opencensus network/statsbeat behaviour."""
    import opencensus.ext.azure.log_exporter as az_log_exporter

    monkeypatch.setattr(az_log_exporter, "AzureLogHandler", _FakeAzureLogHandler)
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        get_settings(),
        "applicationinsights_connection_string",
        "InstrumentationKey=00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",
    )
    configure_logging()
    ai = [h for h in std_logging.getLogger().handlers if isinstance(h, _FakeAzureLogHandler)]
    assert ai, "App Insights handler was not attached when a connection string is set"
    handler = ai[0]

    handler.createLock()  # simulate the beat fork re-initialising logging
    assert handler.lock is not None, "createLock() re-nulled the lock — beat would crash"

    # The exact prod crash path: handle() acquires `with self.lock`. Don't export.
    monkeypatch.setattr(handler, "emit", lambda record: None)
    record = std_logging.LogRecord(
        "celery.beat", std_logging.INFO, __file__, 1, "beat: Starting...", None, None
    )
    handler.handle(record)  # must not raise


def test_app_insights_handler_redacts_foreign_record_secret(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, _restore_root_logging: None
) -> None:
    """#494: the AzureLogHandler must carry the redacting ProcessorFormatter, so a
    FOREIGN (non-structlog) record whose message embeds a secret (e.g. a URL with
    ?token=) is scrubbed before it reaches App Insights — not just the stdout path."""
    import opencensus.ext.azure.log_exporter as az_log_exporter

    monkeypatch.setattr(az_log_exporter, "AzureLogHandler", _FakeAzureLogHandler)
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        get_settings(),
        "applicationinsights_connection_string",
        "InstrumentationKey=00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",
    )
    configure_logging()
    ai = next(h for h in std_logging.getLogger().handlers if isinstance(h, _FakeAzureLogHandler))
    assert ai.formatter is not None, "AI handler must use the redacting ProcessorFormatter"

    record = std_logging.LogRecord(
        "uvicorn.error",
        std_logging.INFO,
        __file__,
        1,
        'GET /api/v1/orchestration/events/adf?token=SUPERSECRET-1 HTTP/1.1" 200',
        None,
        None,
    )
    rendered = ai.format(record)
    assert "SUPERSECRET-1" not in rendered
    assert "token=<redacted>" in rendered
input.new_string:
# ── OTel log export bridge (#524 — replaced the opencensus AzureLogHandler) ──


def _otel_bridge_handler(root: std_logging.Logger) -> std_logging.Handler | None:
    """The OTel LoggingHandler attached to `root` by configure_logging(), if any."""
    from opentelemetry.sdk._logs import LoggingHandler

    return next((h for h in root.handlers if isinstance(h, LoggingHandler)), None)


def _flush_bridge(root: std_logging.Logger) -> None:
    """Drain the bridge handler's BatchLogRecordProcessor to its exporter."""
    handler = _otel_bridge_handler(root)
    assert handler is not None, "OTel log bridge was not attached"
    handler._logger_provider.force_flush()  # type: ignore[attr-defined]


@pytest.fixture
def in_memory_log_exporter(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, _restore_root_logging: None
) -> Any:
    """Route the OTel log bridge to an in-memory exporter (no network) and stub the
    process-global set_logger_provider so repeated configure_logging() calls across
    the suite don't warn/leak a provider."""
    import opentelemetry._logs as otel_logs_api
    from opentelemetry.sdk._logs.export import InMemoryLogRecordExporter

    from backend.app.core import otel as otel_mod

    exporter = InMemoryLogRecordExporter()
    monkeypatch.setattr(otel_mod, "build_log_exporters", lambda settings=None: [exporter])
    monkeypatch.setattr(otel_logs_api, "set_logger_provider", lambda provider: None)
    return exporter


def test_otel_bridge_redacts_foreign_message_body(in_memory_log_exporter: Any) -> None:
    """#494 parity on OTel: a FOREIGN (non-structlog) record whose message embeds a
    secret is scrubbed in the exported BODY before it leaves the process — the
    LoggingHandler renders the body through the redacting ProcessorFormatter."""
    configure_logging()
    root = std_logging.getLogger()
    std_logging.getLogger("uvicorn.error").info(
        'GET /api/v1/orchestration/events/adf?token=SUPERSECRET-1 HTTP/1.1" 200'
    )
    _flush_bridge(root)
    bodies = " ".join(str(log.log_record.body) for log in in_memory_log_exporter.get_finished_logs())
    assert "SUPERSECRET-1" not in bodies
    assert "token=<redacted>" in bodies


def test_otel_bridge_redacts_exported_attributes(in_memory_log_exporter: Any) -> None:
    """The OTel LoggingHandler exports every non-reserved record var as an OTel
    attribute, BYPASSING the body formatter — so a secret in a record `extra=` must
    be scrubbed on the ATTRIBUTE too. This is stricter than the old opencensus
    handler, which only exported the formatted message (#494/#536)."""
    configure_logging()
    root = std_logging.getLogger()
    std_logging.getLogger("some.lib").warning(
        "sample", extra={"password": "hunter2", "order_id": "ORD-1"}
    )
    _flush_bridge(root)
    attrs: dict[str, object] = {}
    for log in in_memory_log_exporter.get_finished_logs():
        attrs.update(dict(log.log_record.attributes or {}))
    assert attrs.get("password") == "<redacted>"
    assert attrs.get("order_id") == "ORD-1"  # non-secret extra preserved verbatim


def test_otel_bridge_handler_has_working_lock_and_handles(in_memory_log_exporter: Any) -> None:
    """#405-class fork-safety guard, ported to OTel. The opencensus crash was
    `createLock()` nulling `self.lock` → `with self.lock` raising on the first emit,
    killing beat (and every periodic task) on its 'beat: Starting...' line. The stdlib
    LoggingHandler keeps a real RLock, and the SDK's BatchLogRecordProcessor re-inits
    its export thread across fork (os.register_at_fork) — so that crash class cannot
    recur. Assert the handler carries a lock and handling a record does not raise."""
    configure_logging()
    handler = _otel_bridge_handler(std_logging.getLogger())
    assert handler is not None
    assert handler.lock is not None, "bridge handler has no lock — emit would crash"
    record = std_logging.LogRecord(
        "celery.beat", std_logging.INFO, __file__, 1, "beat: Starting...", None, None
    )
    handler.handle(record)  # must not raise


def test_no_otel_bridge_when_telemetry_off(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, _restore_root_logging: None
) -> None:
    """No exporter configured (no App Insights connection string) ⇒ no OTel bridge
    attaches; the stdout StreamHandler still carries logs."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(get_settings(), "applicationinsights_connection_string", None)
    configure_logging()
    assert _otel_bridge_handler(std_logging.getLogger()) is None

content.text: Now expand `otel.py` to add the OTLP branches:

input.content:
"""Shared OpenTelemetry exporter resolution — the vendor-neutral observability seam (ADR 0010).

Both signal pipelines resolve their backends here from settings so they can't
drift:

- **tracing.py** — request/task spans (a ``BatchSpanProcessor`` per exporter).
- **logging.py** — the structlog → stdlib → OTel log bridge (#524, which replaced
  the EOL opencensus ``AzureLogHandler``).

Two exporters sit behind the seam and **may both be active at once**:

- **Azure Monitor** — when ``APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING`` is set.
- **Generic OTLP/HTTP** — when ``OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`` is set (#589): spans
  and logs also export to any OTLP consumer (Grafana/Tempo, Jaeger, Datadog, …).
  Base-endpoint semantics per the OTel spec — ``/v1/traces`` and ``/v1/logs`` are
  appended to the configured base. Running both at once is exactly the parity
  check (the same trace/log lands in App Insights AND a local collector).

Neither set ⇒ telemetry is a complete no-op (the pre-#524 ``AzureLogHandler`` gate).

All exporter imports are **lazy** (repo convention, see ``secrets.py``) so a
deployment that uses neither backend never pays the import cost.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

from backend.app.core.config import Settings, get_settings

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from opentelemetry.sdk._logs.export import LogRecordExporter
    from opentelemetry.sdk.resources import Resource
    from opentelemetry.sdk.trace.export import SpanExporter


def _otlp_base(settings: Settings) -> str | None:
    """The OTLP base endpoint (no trailing slash), or None when OTLP is off."""
    endpoint = settings.otel_exporter_otlp_endpoint
    return endpoint.rstrip("/") if endpoint else None


def telemetry_enabled(settings: Settings | None = None) -> bool:
    """True when at least one exporter backend is configured (Azure or OTLP)."""
    settings = settings or get_settings()
    return bool(settings.applicationinsights_connection_string) or bool(_otlp_base(settings))


def build_resource(service_name: str) -> Resource:
    """The OTel Resource carrying ``service.name`` (App Insights cloud role name)."""
    from opentelemetry.sdk.resources import Resource

    return Resource.create({"service.name": service_name})


def build_span_exporters(settings: Settings | None = None) -> list[SpanExporter]:
    """The configured span exporters — Azure Monitor and/or generic OTLP/HTTP."""
    settings = settings or get_settings()
    exporters: list[SpanExporter] = []

    conn = settings.applicationinsights_connection_string
    if conn:
        from azure.monitor.opentelemetry.exporter import AzureMonitorTraceExporter

        exporters.append(AzureMonitorTraceExporter(connection_string=conn))

    base = _otlp_base(settings)
    if base:
        from opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.http.trace_exporter import OTLPSpanExporter

        exporters.append(OTLPSpanExporter(endpoint=f"{base}/v1/traces"))

    return exporters


def build_log_exporters(settings: Settings | None = None) -> list[LogRecordExporter]:
    """The configured log exporters — Azure Monitor and/or generic OTLP/HTTP."""
    settings = settings or get_settings()
    exporters: list[LogRecordExporter] = []

    conn = settings.applicationinsights_connection_string
    if conn:
        from azure.monitor.opentelemetry.exporter import AzureMonitorLogExporter

        exporters.append(AzureMonitorLogExporter(connection_string=conn))

    base = _otlp_base(settings)
    if base:
        from opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.http._log_exporter import OTLPLogExporter

        exporters.append(OTLPLogExporter(endpoint=f"{base}/v1/logs"))

    return exporters

input.old_string:
OTel-neutral core (ADR 0010): the OpenTelemetry SDK + FastAPI/Celery
instrumentations are vendor-neutral; Azure Monitor is one *exporter* behind
the seam (swap the exporter per cloud, nothing else changes). Everything is
gated on ``settings.applicationinsights_connection_string`` — unset ⇒ a
complete no-op, matching ``configure_logging()``'s AzureLogHandler gate.

Deliberately the **exporter-only** package (`azure-monitor-opentelemetry-
exporter`), NOT the `azure-monitor-opentelemetry` distro: the distro
auto-configures the logging pipeline and would collide with the hardened
structlog + opencensus ``AzureLogHandler`` chain in ``logging.py``
(#393/#405 Py3.13 lock fixes). Migrating the *log* pipeline opencensus→OTel
is tracked in #524; this module owns spans only.

All OTel/Azure imports are lazy (repo convention, see ``secrets.py``) so
deployments without App Insights never pay the import cost.
input.new_string:
OTel-neutral core (ADR 0010): the OpenTelemetry SDK + FastAPI/Celery
instrumentations are vendor-neutral; the exporter backends live behind the
shared seam in ``otel.py`` (Azure Monitor and/or generic OTLP/HTTP — #589),
resolved from settings. Tracing is on when **any** backend is configured
(``otel.telemetry_enabled()``) — unset ⇒ a complete no-op, matching the log
pipeline's gate. Spans and logs (#524) now share the same seam and Resource
(service.name).

Deliberately the **exporter-only** Azure package (`azure-monitor-
opentelemetry-exporter`), NOT the `azure-monitor-opentelemetry` distro: the
distro auto-configures the logging pipeline and would double-configure the
structlog log bridge in ``logging.py``.

All exporter imports are lazy (repo convention, see ``secrets.py``) so
deployments without telemetry never pay the import cost.

def configure_tracing(service_name: str) -> None:
    """Install a TracerProvider exporting to App Insights, or no-op.

    Idempotent per process (the global tracer provider can only be set once);
    safe to call from both the API module scope and the Celery
    worker-process-init signal.
    """
    global _provider
    if _provider is not None:
        return
    conn = get_settings().applicationinsights_connection_string
    if not conn:
        return

    from azure.monitor.opentelemetry.exporter import AzureMonitorTraceExporter
    from opentelemetry import trace
    from opentelemetry.sdk.resources import Resource
    from opentelemetry.sdk.trace import TracerProvider
    from opentelemetry.sdk.trace.export import BatchSpanProcessor

    provider = TracerProvider(resource=Resource.create({"service.name": service_name}))
    provider.add_span_processor(
        BatchSpanProcessor(AzureMonitorTraceExporter(connection_string=conn))
    )
    trace.set_tracer_provider(provider)
    _provider = provider
    log.info("tracing_configured", service_name=service_name)
input.new_string:
def configure_tracing(service_name: str) -> None:
    """Install a TracerProvider exporting to the configured backend(s), or no-op.

    Backends (Azure Monitor and/or generic OTLP/HTTP — #589) come from the shared
    ``otel`` seam. Idempotent per process (the global tracer provider can only be
    set once); safe to call from both the API module scope and the Celery
    worker-process-init signal.
    """
    global _provider
    if _provider is not None:
        return
    settings = get_settings()
    exporters = otel.build_span_exporters(settings)
    if not exporters:
        return

    from opentelemetry import trace
    from opentelemetry.sdk.trace import TracerProvider
    from opentelemetry.sdk.trace.export import BatchSpanProcessor

    provider = TracerProvider(resource=otel.build_resource(service_name))
    for exporter in exporters:
        provider.add_span_processor(BatchSpanProcessor(exporter))
    trace.set_tracer_provider(provider)
    _provider = provider
    log.info("tracing_configured", service_name=service_name, exporters=len(exporters))

def test_configure_tracing_turns_on_with_only_otlp_endpoint(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    """#589: tracing is gated on ANY backend now, not just App Insights — an
    OTLP-only config (no connection string) must still stand up a provider and
    export spans. The OTLP/HTTP exporter is swapped for an in-memory one."""
    exporter = InMemorySpanExporter()
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.http.trace_exporter.OTLPSpanExporter",
        lambda *, endpoint: exporter,
    )
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        tracing,
        "get_settings",
        lambda: SimpleNamespace(
            applicationinsights_connection_string=None,
            otel_exporter_otlp_endpoint="http://collector:4318",
        ),
    )
    tracing.configure_tracing(service_name="dataq-api")
    assert tracing._provider is not None

    app = FastAPI()

    @app.get("/api/v1/ping")
    def ping() -> dict[str, str]:
        return {"pong": "yes"}

    tracing.instrument_fastapi(app)
    assert TestClient(app).get("/api/v1/ping").status_code == 200
    tracing._provider.force_flush()
    assert any(s.kind.name == "SERVER" for s in exporter.get_finished_spans())

Migrates the log pipeline off EOL opencensus onto OpenTelemetry and adds a generic OTLP exporter endpoint, so both signals (spans + logs) export to Azure Monitor **and/or** any OTLP consumer behind one shared seam. v1.1 W2 (portability), ADR 0010.

## #524 — opencensus → OTel logs
- New shared exporter seam `backend/app/core/otel.py` (`telemetry_enabled` / `build_span_exporters` / `build_log_exporters` / `build_resource`), lazy imports.
- `logging.py`: replaces the opencensus `AzureLogHandler` with the OTel `LoggerProvider` + `AzureMonitorLogExporter`, keeping the structlog redacting `ProcessorFormatter` in front.
  - **Body** stays scrubbed (`LoggingHandler` renders it via our formatter).
  - **Attributes** are scrubbed too — a `_get_attributes` override runs the OTel handler's verbatim record-var export through the same PII/secret scrubber. This is *stricter* than the old message-only handler (closes an `extra=` leak vector).
- Drops `opencensus-ext-azure` and the whole `#393/#405 createLock` hardening: the stdlib `LoggingHandler` keeps a real lock and the SDK's `BatchLogRecordProcessor` re-inits its export thread across fork (`os.register_at_fork`), so the beat-killing crash class cannot recur.
- `configure_logging(service_name=…)` shares the App Insights cloud role name (`dataq-api` / `dataq-worker`) with spans.

## #589 — generic OTLP endpoint
- `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` (standard OTel contract) adds an OTLP/HTTP backend for spans **and** logs — base-endpoint semantics (`/v1/traces`, `/v1/logs` appended). Azure is now one impl behind the seam; both may run at once (the parity check).
- `tracing.py` gate + exporters go through the shared seam (`telemetry_enabled`, no longer the App-Insights-only conn-string gate).
- Docs: `deploy/README.md` + `.env.app.prod.example` env-var reference.

## Tests
- New `test_otel.py`: the four backend combinations (none / Azure / OTLP / both) + trailing-slash normalisation.
- `test_logging.py`: opencensus createLock/foreign-record tests replaced with OTel in-memory-exporter equivalents (body redaction, **attribute** redaction, the #405-class lock guard, telemetry-off no-op).
- `test_tracing.py`: OTLP-only-turns-tracing-on case; settings stubs updated.

## Local gate
- ruff / black / mypy (app + tests) / bandit / typecheck-deps-sync: clean.
- Full suite: **1411 passed, 1 skipped, 98.41%**. Changed modules: otel.py 100%, logging.py 100%, config.py 100%, tracing.py 96%.

## Acceptance criteria
- [x] Log export runs on OTel; opencensus removed from the pipeline and requirements
- [x] `#405`-class fork-safety guard still green (ported to OTel)
- [x] `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` routes spans+logs to any OTLP consumer; Azure remains default when only the App-Insights string is set
- [x] Documented in the env-var reference
- [ ] Parity verified in App Insights (+ a local OTLP consumer) — **live-verify after deploy** (Azure window)

Closes #524. Closes #589.

PR #628 is up and CI is green so far (pip-audit clean on the new OTLP dep, bandit, lint, typecheck, secret scans all pass); backend tests + CodeQL + frontend E2E still running. The two review agents (code-reviewer + silent-failure-hunter) are analyzing the diff in the background.

Here's where things stand:

## Shipped this turn (PR #628)
**#524 — opencensus → OTel logs**
- New shared exporter seam `backend/app/core/otel.py` (ADR 0010).
- `logging.py` now bridges stdlib → OTel → `AzureMonitorLogExporter`, keeping the redacting `ProcessorFormatter` in front. Crucially, I added a `_get_attributes` override so **exported OTel attributes are scrubbed too** — the base handler ships every record var verbatim, which would have been a *new* `extra=` leak vector the old message-only handler didn't have.
- Dropped `opencensus-ext-azure` + the entire `#405 createLock` hardening — the stdlib handler keeps a real lock and the SDK re-inits its export thread across fork (`os.register_at_fork`), so that crash class can't recur.

**#589 — generic OTLP endpoint**
- `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` adds an OTLP/HTTP backend for spans **and** logs; Azure is now one impl behind the seam, both can run at once (the parity check). `tracing.py` gate moved to `telemetry_enabled` (Azure **or** OTLP).

**Gate:** full suite 1411 pass / 98.41%; changed modules 96–100%; ruff/black/mypy/bandit/typecheck-deps-sync clean.

I'll wait for the two review agents and the remaining CI jobs, fold in any findings, then merge and move to the live parity-verify (deploy + confirm the same trace/log lands in App Insights, plus a local OTLP collector). I'll pick back up when the reviews land.

<result>I've verified all findings against the actual diff. Here's my audit.

## Error-handling audit — PR #628 (opencensus→OTel log pipeline + generic OTLP)

First, credit where due: this diff genuinely *improves* the redaction posture. The `_RedactingOTelLogHandler._get_attributes` override closes a real leak the old `AzureLogHandler` had (a foreign record's `extra={"password": …}` shipped verbatim as an OTel attribute, bypassing the body formatter), app-level structlog records are redacted upstream *before* `wrap_for_formatter` stores them on the record, and the exported body is rendered through the same redacting `ProcessorFormatter` with no-locals tracebacks. I could not find a path where a secret reaches an exporter un-redacted — the attribute path and the body path both route through `_redact_pii`. That part is solid and tested.

The problems are all in resilience and configuration-visibility, not in the scrubber.

---

### HIGH — Telemetry setup is not isolated; any OTel error crashes API + every worker on startup

**Location:** `backend/app/core/logging.py:99-162` (`_configure_otel_log_export`), called unguarded at `logging.py:222`; blast radius via `main.py:51` (lifespan), `main.py:219` (module scope), `celery_app.py` `setup_logging` + `worker_process_init`.

**Scenario (state → outcome):** Operator sets `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` and a malformed `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS` (parsed by the OTLP exporter constructor), or the pinned `opentelemetry-sdk`/`azure-monitor-*-exporter` versions drift and `LoggerProvider`/exporter/`_RedactingOTelLogHandler` construction raises. `_configure_otel_log_export` has no `try/except`, so the exception propagates out of `configure_logging`, which:
- in the API, aborts the ASGI `lifespan` → app never serves;
- in the worker, raises inside the `setup_logging` and `worker_process_init` signal handlers → worker/child dies → crash-loop.

**Hidden errors this reintroduces:** exactly the #405 blast radius (orchestration polling, scheduled dispatch, gap recovery, purge all stop) — this time as a loud crash-loop instead of a silent stall, but still a full outage caused by a *telemetry* misconfiguration. Note the ordering: the stdout `StreamHandler` is attached at `logging.py:198` and `structlog.configure()` runs at `logging.py:224` — *after* the OTel call — so an OTel exception also skips structlog configuration entirely.

**User impact:** a bad observability env var takes down the product. There is no degradation to stdout-only logging, which is the whole point of an observability sidecar being best-effort.

**Recommendation:** wrap only the bridge attach in a `try/except Exception`, log via the already-attached stdout handler (`logging.getLogger(__name__).error(...)` / a structured `logForDebugging`-equivalent with an error id), and continue. The stdout handler already survives; make telemetry failure degrade to "logs still go to stdout, spans/OTLP off" rather than "process down."

---

### MEDIUM — Bridge attached to the root logger → OTel's own export-failure logs feed back into the pipeline

**Location:** `logging.py:162` (`root.addHandler(handler)`); no exclusion of the `opentelemetry`/`azure` logger namespaces anywhere in `configure_logging`.

**Scenario:** the OTLP collector or App Insights is unreachable (the common failure). The SDK's `BatchLogRecordProcessor`/exporter emits `logger.warning("Failed to export …")` on the `opentelemetry` logger. That record propagates to root → the OTel `LoggingHandler` on root → gets queued for export → the next export also fails → another "failed to export" log → … A self-referential amplification that kicks in *precisely when the backend is down*. It's bounded by the batch interval/queue-drop, so not a tight infinite loop, but it's a log/CPU amplifier and pollutes the very backend you're trying to reach once it recovers.

**Recommendation:** set `logging.getLogger("opentelemetry").propagate = False` (and consider `azure.monitor`) in `configure_logging`, or attach the bridge selectively rather than to root. This is a well-known OTel root-bridge footgun.

---

### MEDIUM — Misconfiguration is silently indistinguishable from "telemetry off"; no positive confirmation for the log bridge

**Location:** `otel.py:build_log_exporters`/`build_span_exporters` return `[]`; `_otlp_base` maps empty/whitespace-ish endpoints to `None`; `_configure_otel_log_export` returns silently at `logging.py:118-119`.

**Scenario:** operator typos the var (`OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPONT=…`) or leaves a trailing empty value, believing telemetry is on. `build_*_exporters` returns `[]` → complete silent no-op. There is *no* log line either way for the log bridge — `tracing.py` at least logs `tracing_configured` on success, but the log pipeline logs nothing on attach and nothing on skip, and neither path logs when disabled.

**User impact:** "my logs never show up in App Insights/Tempo" is undiagnosable from the app's own output — the operator can't tell intentional-off from misconfigured-off.

**Recommendation:** emit one info/debug line at configure time naming the resolved backends (count and type, never the connection string/endpoint secret), and a debug line when telemetry resolves to none. The stdout handler is already attached at that point, so this is safe.

---

### LOW–MEDIUM — OTLP base-path append silently misroutes when a user supplies a full signal path

**Location:** `otel.py` `f"{base}/v1/traces"` / `f"{base}/v1/logs"`, passing `endpoint=` explicitly.

**Scenario:** a user following standard OTel docs sets `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://collector:4318/v1/traces` (already signal-specific) → this code produces `…/v1/traces/v1/traces`. Or they set the standard `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT` env var — which this code ignores (settings only reads the base), and the explicit `endpoint=` kwarg *overrides* it. Either way the exporter posts to a wrong URL; the 404/connection error is swallowed in the background batch thread → spans/logs silently never arrive.

**Recommendation:** document the base-only contract more forcefully in the `.env` example (it's noted, but the double-append case isn't), or validate/reject a `base` that already contains `/v1/`. At minimum, finding #3's confirmation log would surface the effective URL.

---

### LOW — `DeprecationWarning` suppression is category-wide, not message-scoped

**Location:** `logging.py:149-156`.

`warnings.simplefilter("ignore", DeprecationWarning)` suppresses *all* `DeprecationWarning`s during `_RedactingOTelLogHandler(...)` construction, not just the specific "use opentelemetry-instrumentation-logging" nudge. Real over-suppression risk is low — the scope is one constructor call and `catch_warnings()` restores state afterward — but any unrelated deprecation emitted inside that constructor (now or after an SDK bump) vanishes with no trace, and it's looser than the message-scoped `filterwarnings` the project uses in `pyproject.toml`. Prefer `warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", message="...LoggingHandler...", category=DeprecationWarning)`.

---

### LOW — Redaction override is pinned-version-fragile and mypy-blind

**Location:** `logging.py:127-142` (`# type: ignore[misc]`, relying on `LoggingHandler._get_attributes` being a callable `staticmethod`).

The attribute-redaction correctness depends on the SDK's `_get_attributes` staying a staticmethod with today's reserved-attr behavior. Because `LoggingHandler` is `Any` under `follow_imports=skip`, mypy can't catch a signature/kind change on an SDK bump. If a future version makes it an instance method or changes what it returns, the override could raise (→ crash per finding #1) or silently stop redacting attributes. It's correctly tested against the pin today (`test_otel_bridge_redacts_exported_attributes`), so this is a pin-discipline note, not a live defect — keep the OTel deps pinned (they are) and treat this test as the tripwire on any bump.

---

### Answering your specific probes

- **No-op paths (`build_* → []`):** correct as "off," but *not* distinguished from misconfiguration — see MEDIUM #3.
- **Would an OTel/provider error silently kill ALL logging including stdout?** No — it would *loudly crash* API/worker startup (stdout handler is attached first, `structlog.configure` is after and gets skipped). So not a silent stdout-kill, but not resilient either — see HIGH #1; it should degrade to stdout-only.
- **DeprecationWarning suppression over-suppress?** Marginally — category-wide but narrowly scoped and restored — see LOW #5.
- **Secret/PII reaching an exporter un-redacted via an error path?** I found none. Both the body and the exported-attribute paths route through `_redact_pii`; structlog records are redacted before they reach the handler; tracebacks are no-locals. This is the strongest part of the PR.

**Files:** `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/core/logging.py`, `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/core/otel.py`, `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/main.py`, `/Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ/backend/app/worker/celery_app.py`.</result>

The silent-failure review is back with strong findings. The redaction is confirmed solid, but there's a legitimate **HIGH**: telemetry setup isn't isolated, so a bad OTLP endpoint/SDK drift would crash API + worker startup (reintroducing the #405 blast radius as a crash-loop). Plus a real root-logger feedback loop (MEDIUM) and diagnosability gaps. These are worth fixing before merge. Let me read the current function and harden it:

result.output:
99	def _configure_otel_log_export(
100	    root: logging.Logger,
101	    level: int,
102	    formatter: logging.Formatter,
103	    service_name: str,
104	) -> None:
105	    """Bridge stdlib logging → OpenTelemetry → the configured exporter(s) (#524).
106	
107	    Replaces the EOL opencensus ``AzureLogHandler`` (and its Py3.13 ``createLock``
108	    hardening, #393/#405). A **no-op** when no exporter is configured, matching the
109	    old connection-string gate. **Fork-safe**: the SDK's ``BatchLogRecordProcessor``
110	    re-inits its export thread in forked children (celery prefork) via
111	    ``os.register_at_fork``, so attaching here — exactly where the old handler
112	    attached — is correct in the worker as well as the API.
113	
114	    The lazy imports (repo convention, ``secrets.py``) keep telemetry-off
115	    deployments from paying the OTel-logs import cost.
116	    """
117	    exporters = otel.build_log_exporters(settings=get_settings())
118	    if not exporters:
119	        return
120	
121	    import warnings
122	
123	    from opentelemetry._logs import set_logger_provider
124	    from opentelemetry.sdk._logs import LoggerProvider, LoggingHandler
125	    from opentelemetry.sdk._logs.export import BatchLogRecordProcessor
126	
127	    class _RedactingOTelLogHandler(LoggingHandler):  # type: ignore[misc]  # LoggingHandler is Any (follow_imports=skip)
128	        """OTel bridge that redacts the EXPORTED ATTRIBUTES, not just the body.
129	
130	        The body is already scrubbed — ``LoggingHandler._translate`` renders it
131	        through our redacting ``ProcessorFormatter`` (``if self.formatter:``). But
132	        the base ``_get_attributes`` copies every non-reserved log-record var into
133	        the exported OTel attributes verbatim, *bypassing the formatter* — so a
134	        foreign record's ``extra=`` (or a library's custom record attribute) could
135	        ship a secret / PII to the backend un-redacted. Run those attributes
136	        through the same PII/secret scrubber the formatter applies to the body
137	        (#494/#536). This is stricter than the old opencensus handler, which only
138	        exported the formatted message."""
139	
140	        @staticmethod
141	        def _get_attributes(record: logging.LogRecord) -> Any:
142	            return _redact_pii(None, "", dict(LoggingHandler._get_attributes(record)))
143	
144	    provider = LoggerProvider(resource=otel.build_resource(service_name))
145	    for exporter in exporters:
146	        provider.add_log_record_processor(BatchLogRecordProcessor(exporter))
147	    set_logger_provider(provider)
148	
149	    with warnings.catch_warnings():
150	        # The sdk `LoggingHandler` carries a DeprecationWarning nudging toward
151	        # `opentelemetry-instrumentation-logging` — which only injects trace
152	        # context into records; it is NOT an export bridge. The sdk handler IS
153	        # the bridge (it's what the azure-monitor distro uses under the hood).
154	        # Suppress the nudge so it neither spams stdout nor trips `-W error`.
155	        warnings.simplefilter("ignore", DeprecationWarning)
156	        handler = _RedactingOTelLogHandler(level=level, logger_provider=provider)
157	    # Same redacting ProcessorFormatter as stdout, so records exported to the
158	    # backend pass through `_redact_pii` (incl. the secret-string scrubber) — a
159	    # foreign record carrying a secret in its message is scrubbed before export
160	    # (#494). App-level structlog records are already redacted upstream.
161	    handler.setFormatter(formatter)

content.text: Both reviews are in. The code-reviewer found **no Critical/Important** and confirmed the redaction is sound (and strengthened). The silent-failure-hunter found a legitimate **HIGH** (telemetry setup not isolated → a bad OTLP endpoint/SDK drift crashes API+worker startup, reintroducing the #405 blast radius) plus a real root-logger feedback loop and diagnosability gaps. I'll fix HIGH #1, MEDIUM #2/#3, LOW #5, and the reviewer's dead-code note. Let me harden the log bridge:

input.old_string:
The lazy imports (repo convention, ``secrets.py``) keep telemetry-off
    deployments from paying the OTel-logs import cost.
    """
    exporters = otel.build_log_exporters(settings=get_settings())
    if not exporters:
        return

    import warnings

    from opentelemetry._logs import set_logger_provider
    from opentelemetry.sdk._logs import LoggerProvider, LoggingHandler
    from opentelemetry.sdk._logs.export import BatchLogRecordProcessor

    class _RedactingOTelLogHandler(LoggingHandler):  # type: ignore[misc]  # LoggingHandler is Any (follow_imports=skip)
        """OTel bridge that redacts the EXPORTED ATTRIBUTES, not just the body.

        The body is already scrubbed — ``LoggingHandler._translate`` renders it
        through our redacting ``ProcessorFormatter`` (``if self.formatter:``). But
        the base ``_get_attributes`` copies every non-reserved log-record var into
        the exported OTel attributes verbatim, *bypassing the formatter* — so a
        foreign record's ``extra=`` (or a library's custom record attribute) could
        ship a secret / PII to the backend un-redacted. Run those attributes
        through the same PII/secret scrubber the formatter applies to the body
        (#494/#536). This is stricter than the old opencensus handler, which only
        exported the formatted message."""

        @staticmethod
        def _get_attributes(record: logging.LogRecord) -> Any:
            return _redact_pii(None, "", dict(LoggingHandler._get_attributes(record)))

    provider = LoggerProvider(resource=otel.build_resource(service_name))
    for exporter in exporters:
        provider.add_log_record_processor(BatchLogRecordProcessor(exporter))
    set_logger_provider(provider)

    with warnings.catch_warnings():
        # The sdk `LoggingHandler` carries a DeprecationWarning nudging toward
        # `opentelemetry-instrumentation-logging` — which only injects trace
        # context into records; it is NOT an export bridge. The sdk handler IS
        # the bridge (it's what the azure-monitor distro uses under the hood).
        # Suppress the nudge so it neither spams stdout nor trips `-W error`.
        warnings.simplefilter("ignore", DeprecationWarning)
        handler = _RedactingOTelLogHandler(level=level, logger_provider=provider)
    # Same redacting ProcessorFormatter as stdout, so records exported to the
    # backend pass through `_redact_pii` (incl. the secret-string scrubber) — a
    # foreign record carrying a secret in its message is scrubbed before export
    # (#494). App-level structlog records are already redacted upstream.
    handler.setFormatter(formatter)
    root.addHandler(handler)
input.new_string:
The lazy imports (repo convention, ``secrets.py``) keep telemetry-off
    deployments from paying the OTel-logs import cost.

    **Best-effort**: the whole setup is isolated in a try/except so an observability
    misconfig (bad OTLP endpoint/headers, SDK drift) degrades to stdout-only logging
    instead of crashing the API lifespan or the celery signal handlers — a telemetry
    fault must never take the process down (the #405 blast radius). The stdout
    handler is already attached by the time we get here.
    """
    settings = get_settings()
    exporters = otel.build_log_exporters(settings=settings)
    if not exporters:
        return

    try:
        import warnings

        from opentelemetry._logs import set_logger_provider
        from opentelemetry.sdk._logs import LoggerProvider, LoggingHandler
        from opentelemetry.sdk._logs.export import BatchLogRecordProcessor

        class _RedactingOTelLogHandler(LoggingHandler):  # type: ignore[misc]  # LoggingHandler is Any (follow_imports=skip)
            """OTel bridge that redacts the EXPORTED ATTRIBUTES, not just the body.

            The body is already scrubbed — ``LoggingHandler._translate`` renders it
            through our redacting ``ProcessorFormatter`` (``if self.formatter:``).
            But the base ``_get_attributes`` copies every non-reserved log-record var
            into the exported OTel attributes verbatim, *bypassing the formatter* —
            so a foreign record's ``extra=`` (or a library's custom record attribute)
            could ship a secret / PII to the backend un-redacted. Run those
            attributes through the same PII/secret scrubber the formatter applies to
            the body (#494/#536). This is stricter than the old opencensus handler,
            which only exported the formatted message."""

            @staticmethod
            def _get_attributes(record: logging.LogRecord) -> Any:
                return _redact_pii(None, "", dict(LoggingHandler._get_attributes(record)))

        provider = LoggerProvider(resource=otel.build_resource(service_name))
        for exporter in exporters:
            provider.add_log_record_processor(BatchLogRecordProcessor(exporter))
        set_logger_provider(provider)

        with warnings.catch_warnings():
            # The sdk `LoggingHandler` carries a DeprecationWarning nudging toward
            # `opentelemetry-instrumentation-logging` — which only injects trace
            # context into records; it is NOT an export bridge. The sdk handler IS
            # the bridge (it's what the azure-monitor distro uses under the hood).
            # Suppress just that message so an unrelated deprecation still surfaces.
            warnings.filterwarnings(
                "ignore", message=r".*LoggingHandler.*", category=DeprecationWarning
            )
            handler = _RedactingOTelLogHandler(level=level, logger_provider=provider)
        # Same redacting ProcessorFormatter as stdout, so records exported to the
        # backend pass through `_redact_pii` (incl. the secret-string scrubber) — a
        # foreign record carrying a secret in its message is scrubbed before export
        # (#494). App-level structlog records are already redacted upstream.
        handler.setFormatter(formatter)
        root.addHandler(handler)

        # Break the feedback loop: the SDK/exporter log their own "failed to export"
        # warnings on these namespaces; propagated to root they re-enter THIS bridge
        # and get re-queued for export — an amplifier that fires exactly when the
        # backend is unreachable. Keep them off the export pipeline (a well-known
        # OTel root-bridge footgun).
        for noisy in ("opentelemetry", "azure.monitor.opentelemetry"):
            logging.getLogger(noisy).propagate = False

        # Positive confirmation so "my logs never reached the backend" is diagnosable
        # (misconfig looks identical to telemetry-off otherwise). Endpoint is infra
        # config, not a secret; the App Insights connection string is NOT logged.
        logging.getLogger(__name__).info(
            "otel_log_export_configured service=%s exporters=%d azure=%s otlp=%s",
            service_name,
            len(exporters),
            bool(settings.applicationinsights_connection_string),
            settings.otel_exporter_otlp_endpoint or "off",
        )
    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — telemetry is best-effort; never crash startup
        # Degrade to stdout-only rather than take the process down (the #405 blast
        # radius: orchestration polling / scheduled dispatch / gap recovery / purge).
        logging.getLogger(__name__).exception("otel_log_export_setup_failed")

input.new_string:
global _provider
    if _provider is not None:
        return
    settings = get_settings()
    try:
        exporters = otel.build_span_exporters(settings)
        if not exporters:
            return

        from opentelemetry import trace
        from opentelemetry.sdk.trace import TracerProvider
        from opentelemetry.sdk.trace.export import BatchSpanProcessor

        provider = TracerProvider(resource=otel.build_resource(service_name))
        for exporter in exporters:
            provider.add_span_processor(BatchSpanProcessor(exporter))
        trace.set_tracer_provider(provider)
        _provider = provider
        log.info("tracing_configured", service_name=service_name, exporters=len(exporters))
    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — telemetry is best-effort; never crash startup
        # Same posture as the log bridge: an observability misconfig (bad OTLP
        # endpoint/headers, SDK drift) must not crash the API lifespan or the celery
        # worker-init signal. Leave _provider None so instrument_* no-op.
        log.warning("tracing_setup_failed", service_name=service_name, exc_info=True)

def test_no_otel_bridge_when_telemetry_off(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, _restore_root_logging: None
) -> None:
    """No exporter configured (no App Insights connection string) ⇒ no OTel bridge
    attaches; the stdout StreamHandler still carries logs."""
    monkeypatch.setattr(get_settings(), "applicationinsights_connection_string", None)
    configure_logging()
    assert _otel_bridge_handler(std_logging.getLogger()) is None


def test_otel_setup_failure_degrades_to_stdout_never_raises(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, _restore_root_logging: None, capsys: pytest.CaptureFixture[str]
) -> None:
    """#628 review (HIGH): a telemetry misconfig (bad OTLP endpoint/headers, SDK
    drift) must NOT crash configure_logging() — which runs in the API lifespan and
    the celery signal handlers (the #405 blast radius). It degrades to stdout-only:
    no bridge attaches, the failure is logged, and stdout logging keeps working."""
    from backend.app.core import otel as otel_mod

    monkeypatch.setattr(otel_mod, "build_log_exporters", lambda settings=None: [object()])

    def _boom(_service_name: str) -> Any:
        raise RuntimeError("otel resource boom")

    monkeypatch.setattr(otel_mod, "build_resource", _boom)

    configure_logging()  # must not raise
    root = std_logging.getLogger()
    assert _otel_bridge_handler(root) is None  # setup failed → bridge not attached

    std_logging.getLogger("still.alive").warning("post-failure line")
    out = capsys.readouterr().out
    assert "otel_log_export_setup_failed" in out  # the degradation was recorded
    assert "post-failure line" in out  # stdout logging survives

input.command:
cd /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ
SP=/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-arijit-Coding-Python-DataQ/4feb9be8-2ae1-4d50-be97-b9c4cbaead36/scratchpad
mkdir -p $SP
cat > $SP/otelcol.yaml <<'YAML'
receivers:
  otlp:
    protocols:
      http:
        endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4318
exporters:
  debug:
    verbosity: detailed
service:
  telemetry:
    logs:
      level: warn
  pipelines:
    traces:
      receivers: [otlp]
      exporters: [debug]
    logs:
      receivers: [otlp]
      exporters: [debug]
YAML
echo "config written"
docker rm -f dataq-otelcol 2>/dev/null
docker run -d --name dataq-otelcol -p 4318:4318 \
  -v $SP/otelcol.yaml:/etc/otelcol/config.yaml \
  otel/opentelemetry-collector:latest 2>&1 | tail -2
sleep 4
echo "=== collector status ==="
docker ps --filter name=dataq-otelcol --format '{{.Names}} {{.Status}}'
docker logs dataq-otelcol 2>&1 | tail -6

content.text: Collector is up. Now the smoke script that drives the real `configure_logging`/`configure_tracing` at the collector, emitting a secret-bearing log + a span:

input.command:
cd /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ
SP=/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-arijit-Coding-Python-DataQ/4feb9be8-2ae1-4d50-be97-b9c4cbaead36/scratchpad
cat > $SP/otlp_smoke.py <<'PY'
import os, time, logging as std_logging
os.environ["OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT"] = "http://localhost:4318"
os.environ["APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING"] = ""  # Azure off → OTLP-only
os.environ.setdefault("SECRET_STORE", "env")

from backend.app.core.config import get_settings
get_settings.cache_clear()
from backend.app.core import logging as dqlog, tracing

dqlog.configure_logging(service_name="dataq-otlp-smoke")
tracing.configure_tracing(service_name="dataq-otlp-smoke")

log = std_logging.getLogger("otlp.smoke")
# secret in the message string AND in a record extra attribute
log.warning(
    'GET /api/v1/orchestration/events/adf?token=SUPERSECRET-LOCAL HTTP/1.1" 200',
    extra={"password": "hunter2-LOCAL", "order_id": "ORD-9"},
)

from opentelemetry import trace
tracer = trace.get_tracer("otlp.smoke")
with tracer.start_as_current_span("smoke-span"):
    pass

# flush both pipelines
from opentelemetry._logs import get_logger_provider
get_logger_provider().force_flush()
if tracing._provider is not None:
    tracing._provider.force_flush()
time.sleep(2)
print("SMOKE_DONE azure_off otlp=http://localhost:4318")
PY
python $SP/otlp_smoke.py 2>&1 | tail -8
